title: Fix inconsistent create method naming (chat_create_* vs chat_*)
description:
Create methods are named chat_create_direct, chat_create_group, chat_create_channel while other operations follow resource_verb. The create_ infix is redundant because the struct already conveys that it is a create. This is inconsistent with how space_create, space_join work (no resource_verb_noun triple).

Evaluate two options:
A) Collapse to a single chat_create(input: ChatCreateInput) where ChatCreateInput is an enum variant: ChatCreate::Direct { contact_id }, ChatCreate::Group { name, members, ... }, ChatCreate::Channel { space_id, name, ... }
B) Keep separate methods but rename to match the resource: chat_direct(input), chat_group(input), chat_channel(input)

Option A is more idiomatic (one entry point, discriminated by type) but is a bigger change. Option B is a simple rename. Either way the current naming (three different methods with create_ in the middle, mixing with the set_ prefix methods) is inconsistent and confusing for new callers.

Record the design decision (A or B) as a child issue before implementing.
